skill.name: screenshots
skill.description: Generate high-resolution marketing and documentation screenshots for web apps with Playwright automation.
skill.summary: Create retina-quality web app screenshots with guided feature discovery and Playwright scripts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and network patterns, but most are Markdown examples or expected Playwright usage. No evidence found of malicious upload, weak cryptography, prompt injection, or hidden exfiltration. The main publish concern is sensitive credential handling in a temporary script and local screenshot capture of potentially private screens.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Credentials Embedded in Temporary Script
medium_findings.description: The skill asks for a login URL, email or username, and password, then shows a generated script with those values stored in constants. This is legitimate browser automation, but it can expose credentials through local files, terminal history, logs, or accidental sharing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs collection of user credentials and places them in a generated script. No exfiltration is shown, so the risk is sensitive local handling rather than confirmed theft.

medium_findings.title: Automated Screenshot Capture May Include Private Data
medium_findings.description: The Playwright workflow navigates to user-selected app pages and saves screenshots to disk. This is core functionality, but generated images may contain private user data, customer records, tokens, or unreleased product information.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The screenshot capture and save path are explicit. The presence of sensitive page content depends on the target app, so this is a contextual risk.

low_findings.title: Command Examples Require Local Execution
low_findings.description: The skill includes shell commands to check Playwright, create a screenshots directory, run a Node script, remove the temporary script, and inspect image files. These are normal for the stated purpose, but users should review commands before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible Markdown examples and align with screenshot generation. I found no dynamic command construction or malicious shell behavior.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Patterns Appear to Be False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported screen capture upload and weak cryptography alerts are not supported by the reviewed lines. The file contains screenshot instructions, filenames, and descriptive text, not upload logic or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations contain prose or screenshot metadata. I found no code that uploads files and no weak hash or cipher implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Supplied URL Navigation
dangerous_patterns.description: The generated Playwright script navigates to URLs derived from user input and planned screenshot paths. This is expected, but it can browse internal or authenticated applications if the user supplies those targets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The navigation pattern is explicit and required for Playwright screenshots. The security impact depends on the URL and user authorization.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Writes for Screenshot Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill creates a screenshots directory and writes PNG files there. This is expected functionality, but users should ensure outputs do not include confidential information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are directly shown in the script template and verification commands. They are not malicious by themselves.

content.value_statement: Teams need polished screenshots for launches, docs, and social channels. This skill guides feature discovery and generates retina-quality captures with Playwright.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether Playwright is available before generating screenshots.
Guides users through screenshot count, purpose, and authentication needs.
Reviews app documentation and routes to identify useful screens.
Creates a Playwright script with 1440 by 900 viewport and 2x scale.
Captures full-page, element-focused, modal, hover, and dark mode screenshots.
Summarizes generated PNG files, sizes, and expected retina resolution.
limitations: Requires Playwright and a reachable web app URL.
Authentication flows may need custom selectors for unusual login pages.
Screenshots may include sensitive app data if pages are not prepared.
Generated image quality depends on the app state, data, and viewport fit.

use_cases.title: Launch Asset Production
use_cases.description: Capture a concise screenshot set for Product Hunt, social posts, and launch pages.

use_cases.title: Documentation Image Updates
use_cases.description: Regenerate current UI screenshots for guides, onboarding pages, and help center articles.

use_cases.title: Feature Showcase Review
use_cases.description: Explore routes and components, then capture the strongest product screens for review.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Basic Screenshots
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a public app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 3 to 5 marketing screenshots for my app at http://localhost:3000. Use public pages only.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Launch Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Product Hunt screenshot set for my web app. Find key features from the README and routes first.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Authenticated Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Create screenshots for the dashboard and settings pages. Ask me for login details and avoid showing private customer data.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Complete Screenshot Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the app routes, propose 8 screenshot targets, confirm them with me, then capture retina PNG files with Playwright.

output_examples.input: Create five launch screenshots for a Vite app running locally.
output: A confirmed screenshot plan covering dashboard, feature pages, and settings.
Five PNG files saved in the screenshots directory at retina resolution.
A short summary with filenames, dimensions, and file sizes.

output_examples.input: Update documentation screenshots for an authenticated dashboard.
output: Questions about login URL, account details, and pages to capture.
A Playwright capture flow tailored to the discovered routes.
A final list of generated documentation images and any failed selectors.

best_practices: Use demo data and remove private information before capturing screenshots.
Confirm the screenshot list before running browser automation.
Review generated scripts before execution when credentials are required.
anti_patterns: Do not capture production pages that contain customer data or secrets.
Do not paste long-lived credentials into reusable scripts or shared terminals.
Do not rely on screenshots taken before animations and data loading finish.

faq.question: Does this skill require Playwright?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks for Playwright and suggests installation commands if it is missing.

faq.question: Can it capture authenticated pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide login automation when the user provides credentials and a login URL.

faq.question: Where are screenshots saved?
faq.answer: The workflow saves PNG files in a local screenshots directory.

faq.question: Can it capture dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions include a Playwright context option for dark mode screenshots.

faq.question: Does it upload screenshots anywhere?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed file saves screenshots locally and summarizes local files.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing images?
faq.answer: Review every image for private data, tokens, customer records, and unreleased product information.
